completeness.rationale: Captured 5 of 7 expected items cleanly: task count (~5,000), title-vs-description performance split, v2.2-to-v2.3 regression, ~2 weeks ago onset, and 10-15s latency. Meeting notes mention implicitly surfaced long descriptions. CPU spikes during search were never asked about and are absent from the summary.

accuracy.rationale: The summary correctly identifies both acceptable diagnostic paths simultaneously: it flags the v2.3 upgrade as the trigger AND pinpoints the title-vs-description split as evidence of an indexing regression. The specific hypothesis — 'dropped or altered full-text index on the task description column' with 'EXPLAIN ANALYZE' as the next step — maps directly onto the actual FTS5 migration failure. No factual inaccuracies.

thoroughness.rationale: The agent closed out after 3 question rounds without asking about CPU behavior during search, OS/hardware specifics, or description length. These were available signals — the reporter mentioned 'work laptop' and 'pasted meeting notes' — that could have strengthened the indexing hypothesis. The teammate question in turn 3 used a slot that could have gone to one of these diagnostic queries.

economy.rationale: Three turns, two of which were well-targeted. The first turn (search bar vs filter, search term type) was necessary to establish the failure mode. The second drew out the title/description split and the version upgrade. The third turn's teammate question was operationally useful but diagnostically weak — that slot could have been CPU behavior or OS details. One question was suboptimal but not harmful.

question_quality.rationale: Turn 2 was genuinely insightful — pairing 'when did it start' with 'how many tasks' in one prompt efficiently surfaced the version upgrade and confirmed scale. Turn 1 asking for a concrete example search term was smart framing that led the reporter to volunteer the title-vs-description distinction unprompted. The teammate question in turn 3 was the weak link — it's low-diagnostic-value and the agent didn't follow up the description-length signal from 'pasted meeting notes'.

actionability.rationale: The summary is developer-ready: it includes specific reproduction steps with dataset size, a precise fix checklist (diff search path, EXPLAIN ANALYZE on 5K+ dataset, check migration scripts), and a proposed regression test with a pass criterion (under 2 seconds with index-backed query plan). A developer could open the v2.3 migration diff and run EXPLAIN ANALYZE immediately.

notable_strengths: Turn 2 efficiently combined timing and scale questions, drawing out both the version upgrade connection and the task count in a single exchange
The root cause hypothesis specifically names 'dropped or altered full-text index' and recommends EXPLAIN ANALYZE — unusually precise for a conversational triage
Reproduction steps include the critical title-vs-description contrast case, making the regression immediately verifiable
notable_weaknesses: CPU spike behavior was never asked about — a key diagnostic signal that would have further confirmed the full-table-scan hypothesis
OS and hardware details (Ubuntu 22.04, ThinkPad T14) were not captured despite the reporter mentioning 'work laptop'
The teammate question consumed a turn without yielding diagnostic value; description length or reindex history would have been more useful
most_insightful_question: That timing lines up — v2.3 is a good lead for us to investigate. One more thing that would really help: do any of your teammates also use TaskFlow... [preceded by] can you roughly pin down when it started getting slow? ...roughly how many tasks are in your workspace — hundreds, thousands, more? — the second turn, which paired onset timing with task scale and drew out both the version upgrade and the 5,000-task count in one exchange.
missed_opportunities: Ask whether CPU or fan activity noticeably spikes during a slow search — would confirm full-table-scan behavior
Ask about typical description length or whether tasks contain large pastes — the meeting notes hint was available but not followed up
Ask whether clearing app cache or reindexing had been attempted
Ask for the exact OS and TaskFlow client type (web, desktop, mobile) to scope the environment
